# Pr 867001 Introduce Token Stage Cohorting And Route Matrix Alerting

Source type: github
Document ID: dsid_c1a6a71323c04c1ba5445aadea340362
Release: v1.0.0
Benchmark: EnterpriseRAG-Bench synthetic upstream data
introduce-token-stage-cohorting-and-route-matrix-alerting

Context: long-tail token cost and multi-stage latency spikes frequently surface only when a request traverses several model-serving stages (tokenization, encoder, decode, fallback). Existing alerts were per-route and per-quantile but lacked a compact way to group requests by 'stage footprint' and automatically route alerting weight to the stage(s) most likely responsible. Motivation: reduce alert noise and accelerate RCA by grouping invocations into stage-aware cohorts and deriving a route x stage alert matrix that attributes SLO violations to the most likely pipeline stage. What this PR does: 1) Adds a lightweight token_stage_cohort service that computes a deterministic cohort key per invocation based on stage-level metrics (token counts per stage, kv-cache hit pattern, decode-batch size) and stores short-lived cohort aggregates. 2) Introduces an alert route-matrix generator that consumes cohort rollups and emits alert bindings which bias escalation to the top-k stages for that route. 3) Adds a new dashboard focused on cohort drift, stage heatmaps, and per-cohort cost/latency breakdown to help oncall quickly validate root cause hypotheses. 4) Wires the generator into the existing alerting pipeline with a safe canary rollout and automatic quiet-window attenuation to avoid regressing noise. Compatibility and rollout: service defaults to read-only mode until flagged by a feature flag; alert generation starts cross-account in a low-sensitivity mode (only P90/P99) and is then ramped after 72h of monitoring. Testing: unit and integration tests added; ran a 48h synthetic replay against production-like traces (see test run in CI). Checklist: - [x] Unit tests - [x] Integration tests - [x] Dashboard panels - [x] Docs for oncall and runbook - [x] Feature-flagged rollout Plan for operators: deploy to staging -> enable feature flag -> monitor cohort drift and false-positive rate -> bump sensitivity -> full rollout. Links: Linear ENG-1248 (cohorting design) and PM-432 (dashboard spec).
Diego: Can cohort keys be stable across model version rollouts? Added suggestion to include model-version hash in key derivation. Response: we now namespace cohort keys with model-major-version and include fallback mapping in cohort_store.proto.
Priya: Alert noise risk—please limit initial sensitivity and provide quiet-window behavior. Response: implemented quiet-window attenuation and low-sensitivity defaults; documented in docs/observability/token-cohorting.md.
Liam: test coverage looks good but add an e2e replay with partial KV-cache misses. Response: added synthetic KV miss replay in tests/integration/test_cohorting_flow.py and CI run shows stable results.
